Class App

java.lang.Object
io.github.isoyigido.basic.gui.app.App
public final class App extends Object

Stores application appearance.

Includes:

  • getColorProfile()
  • getFont(float)
See Also:
  • ColorProfile
  • Font

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static ColorProfile
    getColorProfile()
    Returns the current color profile of the application.
    static Font
    getFont(float size)
    Returns the current text font of the application with the given font size.
    static void
    setColorProfile(ColorProfile colorProfile)
    Sets the color profile of the application to the given color profile.
    static void
    setFont(Font font)
    Sets the text font of the application to the given text font.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • getColorProfile

      public static ColorProfile getColorProfile()
      Returns the current color profile of the application.
      Returns:
      the current color profile
      See Also:
      • ColorProfile

    • setColorProfile

      public static void setColorProfile(ColorProfile colorProfile)
      Sets the color profile of the application to the given color profile.
      Parameters:
      colorProfile - the new color profile to be set
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the input colorProfile is null
      See Also:
      • ColorProfile

    • getFont

      public static Font getFont(float size)
      Returns the current text font of the application with the given font size.
      Parameters:
      size - the font size (in points)
      Returns:
      the current text font with the given font size
      See Also:
      • Font
      Implementation Note:
      This method uses Font.deriveFont(int, float) to derive a plain font with the given size from the current font.

    • setFont

      public static void setFont(Font font)
      Sets the text font of the application to the given text font.
      Parameters:
      font - the new text font to be set
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if the input font is null
      See Also:
      • Font
